title: capparis spinosa subsp. sicula
intro: Capparis spinosa subsp. sicula, also known as Capers, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Capparaceae family and is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Capparis spinosa subsp. sicula include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting cardiovascular health, among others. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in culinary dishes, particularly in Mediterranean cuisine, where its buds and leaves are pickled and used as a condiment.
